# Introduction
The current system supports two scenarios, namely the Virtual Power Plant scenario and the Northbound scenario. The specific introductions are as follows:
- **VPP (Virtual Power Plant) Scene**: In this scene, the equipment is controlled by the service provider, and the owner has no right to switch the operation mode of the power plant. At the same time, the system supports the service provider to subscribe to and actively query the data of the power plant.
- **NBI (North Bound) Scene**: In this scene, both the owner and the service provider can actively switch the operation mode of the power plant. When the service provider needs to control the power plant, it can switch to the Northbound mode. If the owner wishes to switch to the Time-of-Use (TOU) mode or the full-scale power grid connection mode, the operation can also be carried out. In addition, the system also supports the service provider to actively query the data.

# Supplementary Notes on VPP Scene
At present, the VPP mode is only supported for use by service providers who possess the secret key. Once the service provider executes the onboard interface, they will acquire the operational authority over the power station, and this operation will also switch the power station to run in the VPP mode. In this mode, the owner has no right to switch to other modes.
After the onboard operation is carried out, if the service provider requires the device to report the operation data, they can subscribe to the device data (for specific operation steps, please refer to [Subscription](https://developer.sigencloud.com/user/api/document/44)). Upon receiving the subscription information from the service provider, the power station will report the corresponding data according to the subscribed data type. Detailed descriptions of different subscription types can be found in [Telemetry](https://developer.sigencloud.com/user/api/document/51), [System Data](https://developer.sigencloud.com/user/api/document/52), and [Alarm](https://developer.sigencloud.com/user/api/document/54). Moreover, the service provider can also issue commands targeting the power station, and the specific content is elaborated in [Instruction](https://developer.sigencloud.com/user/api/document/55). If the service provider needs to actively query the data of the power station, they can refer to the relevant interface instructions in [Inventory](https://developer.sigencloud.com/user/api/document/28), [Historical Data](https://developer.sigencloud.com/user/api/document/32), and [RealTime Data](https://developer.sigencloud.com/user/api/document/35).
The owner cannot actively exit the VPP scenario, and it is necessary for the service provider to perform the exit operation. Once the VPP scenario is exited, the power station will depart from the VPP mode and switch to the maximum self-generation and self-consumption mode. After exiting the VPP scenario, the service provider will no longer be able to carry out any of the operations mentioned above on the power station.

# Supplementary Notes on NBI Scene
The Northbound scenario supports two methods for obtaining tokens: using a secret key and using an account and password. The main difference between the two methods lies in the power station authorization process. Customers using a secret key must go through the onboard authorization process, while those using an account and password do not need to execute the onboard process. For specific differences, please refer to the following figure.
It should be noted that in the Northbound scenario, only the query of power station data is supported by default. For other functions, you need to apply to technical personnel for access permissions.

![scene](https://sigen-data-cn-public.s3.cn-northwest-1.amazonaws.com.cn//scene11.jpg)
